response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software engineer with a strong track record in web platforms, low-latency services, and developer tooling, but has no discernible background in SoC or IP modeling, hardware architecture, AI accelerator design, GPU workload analysis, or deep neural network training and inference optimization. The Principal Performance Architect role is fundamentally a hardware-software co-design position requiring chip-level expertise; the candidate's experience is entirely in application and systems software. There is no overlap with the core required skills beyond a general familiarity with performance-conscious software engineering.
strengths: Demonstrated performance-sensitive engineering at scale, including high-throughput, low-latency micro-services handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby.
Broad multi-language and multi-platform experience (C/C++, DLang, TypeScript, embedded systems) indicating adaptability and systems-level thinking.
Recent involvement in AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq shows awareness of AI ecosystem trends.
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, which is a non-negotiable core requirement for this role.
No background in hardware architecture (GPU, SIMD, MIMD, AI accelerators) or hardware-software co-design at the chip or system level.
No experience with deep learning training or inference optimization, performance profiling of DNN workloads, or any ML framework at the infrastructure/hardware layer.

key_factors.factor: No hardware architecture or AI accelerator background
